title: The Investor-Ready D2C & Ecommerce Financial Model
subtitle: Know Exactly When to Reorder. Prove Unit Economics to Investors.
descriptionBody: "When do we reorder inventory?" "Are our Facebook ads actually profitable after COGS, shipping, and returns?" Running a consumer brand means cash flow is king and inventory is the silent killer. Most founders fly blind on reorder points, or they try to force their brand into a generic SaaS spreadsheet — a guaranteed way to stock out of your hero product or burn runway on unprofitable ad campaigns. This model solves both.
section1Body: This is a battle-tested, clean-code 3-year projection engine built specifically for modern consumer brands. It handles the heavy lifting of traffic conversion, cohort retention, inventory forecasting, and valuation so you can stop wrestling with spreadsheets and get back to building your brand.

features.name: Inventory Cash Flow Radar
features.desc: Never stock out again. Input your safety stock and lead times; the model tells you exactly when to reorder and how much cash it drains.

features.name: Ad Profitability Calculator
features.desc: Input your ad spend, ROAS, COGS, and shipping. See true contribution margin per channel, not just revenue.

features.name: 36-Month Cohort Tracker
features.desc: Watch repeat purchase rates by acquisition month. Calculate real LTV for subscription and subscription-adjacent brands.

features.name: Multi-Channel Revenue Engine
features.desc: Forecast D2C, Wholesale, Bundles, and Subscriptions separately with distinct AOVs for each.

features.name: D2C Valuation Bridge
features.desc: Revenue Multiples + DCF tailored for consumer brands, not SaaS metrics.

whyPoints.title: Never Stock Out of Your Hero Product
whyPoints.desc: Input safety stock and lead times; get automated reorder alerts before you lose a sale.

whyPoints.title: Know Your True Ad Profitability
whyPoints.desc: Most founders calculate ROAS wrong. This model subtracts COGS, shipping, and returns to show real contribution margin.

whyPoints.title: Prove Unit Economics to Investors
whyPoints.desc: VCs and banks for consumer brands care about inventory turns and contribution margins, not just ARR. Show them you understand your business.

footerSummaryDetails: (Cheaper than stocking out of your hero product).
footerResultDetails: Total clarity on your brand's financial future and inventory needs.
whatYouGet: A complete, unlocked 3-year financial projection spreadsheet (Excel format).
Traffic & Conversion Tracker to automatically calculate your Blended CAC.
Fully automated 36-month cohort retention matrix to track repeat purchases.
Inventory Cash Flow Planner with automated reorder forecasting.
Step-by-step "Start Here" manual to plug in your numbers in under 30 minutes.
whoThisIsFor: D2C founders preparing for investor pitch or bank loan
Consumer brands with physical inventory (not dropshipping)
Brands running paid ads who need to prove unit economics

faq.q: Does this work for dropshipping, or just for brands holding inventory?
faq.a: Both! If you hold physical inventory, the built-in Inventory Cash Flow Planner will track your reorder points and cash burn. If you dropship, you can simply zero out the inventory lead times and just use the multi-channel growth and cohort retention engines.

faq.q: I'm not a finance person. Is this too complex?
faq.a: Not at all. I survived the trenches of CA prep so you don't have to. Just follow the step-by-step "Start Here" guide and plug your numbers into the blue cells. The model automatically does the heavy lifting.

faq.q: Can I forecast subscriptions and standard purchases at the same time?
faq.a: Yes. The Multi-Channel Growth Engine lets you separate and forecast standard one-off D2C orders, recurring subscriptions, product bundles, and even wholesale orders all in one place.
